metadata.summary: IaC CI/CD pipeline patterns: GitHub Actions workflow with format/validate/lint/security/plan/apply stages, OIDC authentication, drift detection, and production approval gates.

content.title: IaC CI/CD Integration Patterns
content.summary: CI/CD pipeline patterns for Terraform: a six-stage GitHub Actions workflow (fmt/validate/lint/security/plan/apply), OIDC for credential-free cloud authentication, PR plan comments for review, environment-specific apply jobs with approval gates, and scheduled drift detection.

why.p: Manual terraform apply without a pipeline gate is the single largest source of infrastructure incidents: stale plans applied after force-pushes, no peer review of the plan output, no audit trail. A CI/CD pipeline enforces the review-before-apply contract, uses short-lived OIDC tokens instead of long-lived IAM keys, and provides an audit log of every apply. Drift detection closes the loop by alerting when infrastructure diverges from the declared state.

list.item: Any Terraform project with more than one contributor — enforce plan-before-apply via CI.
Infrastructure with compliance or audit requirements — CI provides an immutable apply log.
Multi-environment deployments (dev/staging/prod) — use matrix strategy to plan all environments on PR.
Production infrastructure — add approval gates before apply on merge to main.
Long-lived infrastructure — add scheduled drift detection to catch out-of-band changes.
Throwaway or one-time environments — pipeline setup overhead exceeds value for ephemeral infra.
Local development inner loop — run fmt/validate locally; reserve CI for pre-merge gates.
Drift detection when environment changes are expected (active development phase) — generates false-positive noise.

rule.statement: The CI pipeline MUST run: (1) fmt -check, (2) validate, (3) tflint, (4) security scan (checkov/tfsec), (5) plan on PR with comment output, (6) apply only on merge to main. Apply MUST require all previous stages to pass.
rule.rationale: Each stage catches a different failure class: fmt catches formatting drift, validate catches syntax errors, tflint catches provider-specific issues, security scan catches policy violations, and the plan review is the human gate before any cloud mutation.

rule.statement: Cloud authentication in CI MUST use OIDC (id-token: write permission + configure-aws-credentials with role-to-assume). NEVER store long-lived IAM access keys as CI secrets.
rule.rationale: OIDC tokens are short-lived (15 minutes) and scoped to the specific workflow run; a leaked token has minimal blast radius compared to a long-lived IAM key that persists until manually rotated.

rule.statement: Production apply jobs MUST use a GitHub Environments deployment gate requiring manual approval. Apply from main branch only; never allow direct apply from feature branches.
rule.rationale: An approval gate before production apply is the last human checkpoint; it prevents accidental production deploys from merged-but-unreviewd PRs.

example.title: Complete GitHub Actions Terraform workflow
example.code:
# .github/workflows/terraform.yml name: Terraform
on: pull_request: paths: - 'infrastructure/**' push: branches: - main paths: - 'infrastructure/**'
permissions: id-token: write contents: read pull-requests: write
env: TF_VERSION: "1.6.0" AWS_REGION: "us-east-1"
jobs: validate: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4
- uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3 with: terraform_version: ${{ env.TF_VERSION }}
- name: Terraform Format run: terraform fmt -check -recursive working-directory: infrastructure
- name: Terraform Init run: terraform init -backend=false working-directory: infrastructure/environments/dev
- name: Terraform Validate run: terraform validate working-directory: infrastructure/environments/dev
lint: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4
- uses: terraform-linters/setup-tflint@v4
- name: TFLint run: | tflint --init tflint --recursive
security: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4
- name: Checkov uses: bridgecrewio/checkov-action@v12 with: directory: infrastructure framework: terraform output_format: sarif
plan: needs: [validate, lint, security] runs-on: ubuntu-latest strategy: matrix: environment: [dev, staging, prod] steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4
- uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3 with: terraform_version: ${{ env.TF_VERSION }}
- name: Configure AWS Credentials uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4 with: role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::${{ secrets.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}:role/TerraformCI aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}
- name: Terraform Init run: terraform init working-directory: infrastructure/environments/${{ matrix.environment }}
- name: Terraform Plan id: plan run: terraform plan -no-color -out=tfplan working-directory: infrastructure/environments/${{ matrix.environment }}
- name: Comment Plan if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' uses: actions/github-script@v7 with: script: | const output = `#### Terraform Plan - ${{ matrix.environment }} \`\`\` ${{ steps.plan.outputs.stdout }} \`\`\``; github.rest.issues.createComment({ issue_number: context.issue.number, owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo, body: output })
apply: needs: plan if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' runs-on: ubuntu-latest environment: production steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4
- uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3 with: terraform_version: ${{ env.TF_VERSION }}
- name: Configure AWS Credentials uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4 with: role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::${{ secrets.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}:role/TerraformCI aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}
- name: Terraform Apply run: | terraform init terraform apply -auto-approve working-directory: infrastructure/environments/prod

section.name: makefile-template
section.p: A local Makefile for developer workflow — mirrors the CI stages for consistent local execution:

example.title: Makefile for Terraform operations
example.code:
# Makefile for Terraform operations
ENV ?= dev MODULE ?= .
.PHONY: init plan apply destroy fmt validate lint test
init: cd environments/$(ENV) && terraform init
plan: cd environments/$(ENV) && terraform plan
apply: cd environments/$(ENV) && terraform apply
destroy: cd environments/$(ENV) && terraform destroy
fmt: terraform fmt -recursive
validate: @for env in environments/*/; do \ echo "Validating $$env..."; \ cd $$env && terraform init -backend=false && terraform validate && cd ../..; \ done
lint: tflint --recursive
security: checkov -d . --framework terraform
test: cd modules/$(MODULE)/tests && terraform test
test-all: @for module in modules/*/; do \ if [ -d "$$module/tests" ]; then \ echo "Testing $$module..."; \ cd $$module/tests && terraform test && cd ../../..; \ fi \ done
docs: @for module in modules/*/; do \ terraform-docs markdown $$module > $$module/README.md; \ done

section.name: drift-detection
section.p: Drift detection runs terraform plan on a schedule and alerts when the plan is non-empty (resources have drifted from declared state):

rule.statement: Configure a scheduled drift detection job that runs terraform plan daily (or hourly for critical infrastructure) and alerts when plan output is non-empty. Do NOT auto-apply drift remediation — require human review of each drift incident.
rule.rationale: Auto-remediation of drift can destroy intentional manual changes made during an incident response. Alert and require human judgment for each drift detection event.

checklist.check: Credentials from vault/secrets manager — no long-lived IAM keys in CI secrets
OIDC for cloud authentication (id-token: write permission)
Least privilege for pipeline service account (plan vs apply roles separate)
No secrets in state (use data sources for runtime secrets)
Audit logging enabled for all apply operations
Production apply requires manual approval gate
PR requires plan output for review

section.name: llm-prompts-cicd
section.prompt:
Create a CI/CD pipeline for Terraform using [GitHub Actions/GitLab CI/Jenkins]:
Requirements: 1. Format check on PRs 2. Validation and linting 3. Security scanning (checkov/tfsec) 4. Plan on PR with comment output 5. Apply on merge to main 6. Environment-specific workflows
Security requirements: - OIDC authentication (no long-lived credentials) - Approval gates for production - Audit logging
Provide complete workflow file with comments.

Set up drift detection for Terraform-managed infrastructure:
Requirements: - Scheduled runs (frequency: [SCHEDULE]) - Alert on detected drift - Option to auto-remediate or report only - Integration with [ALERTING_SYSTEM]
Provide: 1. Workflow/job configuration 2. Drift detection script 3. Alerting configuration 4. Remediation options

Help debug this failed Terraform apply:
Error message: [PASTE_ERROR]
Configuration: [PASTE_RELEVANT_CONFIG]
Context: - Environment: [ENV] - Last successful apply: [TIMESTAMP] - Recent changes: [DESCRIBE_CHANGES]
Provide: 1. Root cause analysis 2. Potential solutions ranked by likelihood 3. Verification steps 4. Prevention recommendations

Step 1: Analyze requirements "Analyze these requirements for a [MODULE_TYPE] module: [REQUIREMENTS] Identify: inputs needed, outputs expected, resources to create, edge cases."
Step 2: Design module interface "Based on the analysis, design the module interface: - variables.tf with types, defaults, validation - outputs.tf with descriptions - Example usage in README"
Step 3: Implement module "Implement main.tf following these patterns: [REFERENCE_PATTERNS] Include: resource definitions, locals, data sources"
Step 4: Add tests "Write tests for the module covering: - Happy path with defaults - All optional parameters - Validation failures - Integration verification"
Step 5: Documentation "Complete the README with: - Purpose and use cases - Requirements table - Input/output tables - Examples (basic and advanced) - Related modules"
